raw_consumer_statement: I purchased a wig from a wig merchant for XXXX in XX/XX/year>. Two months after my purchase, the wig fell apart despite being advertised to last 5 years and after being repaired twice. I tried to return the wig to the merchant and they refused, so I disputed with my AMEX. When filing the original dispute, I was never prompted to upload any documentation and only given a text box to describe my issue. Without asking any more information from me my dispute was closed, citing that the merchant claimed I violated " Terms and Conditions '' by putting lace melting spray ( an industry used wig adhesive ) on the wig and that they offered to repair the wig -- which was included in a PDF the merchant sent. However, it is clear that AMEX did not actually look into the dispute details because part of the screenshots the merchant provided were not even from me- it was from a customer with the same issue ( page 28-31 on the document entitled Merchant dispute document ), and the terms and conditions do not state anywhere to not use lace melting spray ( Merchant dispute document pages 4-5 ). During the reopening of the disputre, AMEX called me once asking for more information for the dispute, and I had missed the call and tried calling back 4 times and left a message every time. I never spoke with anyone after that before they closed the dispute. I believe AMEX did not actually look into the dispute or they would have seen that merchant 's arguments were not valid to my dispute, which was I had been sold a faulty product. I have attached the merchant 's dispute argument and what I had submitted to AMEX which was never addressed before the dispute was closed out. I look forward to hearing from you.
